title: Selenicereus hondurensis
intro: Selenicereus hondurensis, also known as the Honduran night-blooming cereus, is a species of cactus native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Bioactive constituents of Selenicereus hondurensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications for skin issues and wounds.
taxonomy: Selenicereus hondurensis is a species of flowering cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a part of the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Hylocereeae. Selenicereus hondurensis is also known as the Honduran queen of the night or the Honduran night-blooming cereus. This plant is native to the tropical forests of Honduras and has white funnel-shaped flowers that bloom at night. It is a relatively rare species that is highly valued by collectors for its unique and fragrant blooms.
uses: Selenicereus hondurensis has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its stems and flowers are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. Additionally, its extracts are used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

side_effects: Selenicereus hondurensis, also known as the Honduran night-blooming cereus or queen of the night, is a flowering cactus that is native to Central America. Due to its low toxicity, it is not considered to be highly hazardous to humans or animals. However, ingestion of the plant's seeds or other parts can cause gastrointestinal irritation and other mild side effects in some individuals. Moreover, it is also possible for people to experience allergic reactions or skin irritation from touching the plant's spines. If ingested in large quantities, Selenicereus hondurensis may cause more severe symptoms, but this is generally rare.
